comment: Claude Code prompt library, mirrored from https://code.claude.com/docs/en/prompt-library (52 prompts). The page is rendered client-side, so these are bundled into the binary rather than scraped at runtime. category = phase tag; tags = source group.

prompts.title: Get oriented in a new repository
prompts.content: give me an overview of this codebase: architecture, key directories, and how the pieces connect

prompts.title: Explain unfamiliar code
prompts.content: explain what src/scheduler/queue.ts does and how data flows through it. write it up as an HTML page with a diagram, then open it in my browser

prompts.title: Find where something happens
prompts.content: where do we validate uploaded file types?

prompts.title: Check what breaks before you delete
prompts.content: what would break if I deleted the retryWithBackoff helper?

prompts.title: Trace how code evolved
prompts.content: look through the commit history of internal/auth/session.go and summarize how it evolved and why

prompts.title: Scope a change before you start
prompts.content: which files would I need to touch to add a dark mode toggle to settings?

prompts.title: Ask the codebase a product question
prompts.content: I am a PM. walk me through what happens when a user clicks Export to PDF, from the UI down to the result

prompts.title: Plan a multi-file change before touching code
prompts.content: plan how to refactor the payment module to support multiple currencies. list the files you would change, but don't edit anything yet

prompts.title: Draft a spec by interview
prompts.content: I want to build per-workspace rate limits. interview me about implementation, UX, edge cases, and tradeoffs until we have covered everything, then write the spec to SPEC.md

prompts.title: Turn a meeting into tickets
prompts.content: read @meeting-notes.md and write up the action items, then create a Linear ticket for each with acceptance criteria

prompts.title: Map edge cases before building
prompts.content: list the error states, empty states, and edge cases for the file upload flow that the design needs to cover

prompts.title: Turn a mockup into a working prototype
prompts.content: here is a mockup. build a working prototype I can click through, matching the layout and states shown

prompts.title: Implement from a screenshot and self-check
prompts.content: implement this design, then take a screenshot of the result, compare it to the original, and fix any differences

prompts.title: Follow an existing pattern
prompts.content: look at how the GitHub webhook handler is implemented to understand the pattern, then build a Stripe webhook handler the same way

prompts.title: Generate docs for undocumented code
prompts.content: find the public functions in src/auth/ without JSDoc comments and add them, matching the style already used in the file

prompts.title: Add a small, well-defined feature
prompts.content: add a /health endpoint that returns the app version and uptime

prompts.title: Build a small internal tool from scratch
prompts.content: create a drag-and-drop Kanban board with three columns using HTML, CSS, and vanilla JavaScript, then open it in my browser

prompts.title: Work an issue end to end
prompts.content: read issue #312, implement the fix, and run the tests

prompts.title: Find and update copy across the codebase
prompts.content: find every place we say "Sign up free" or a close variant, show me each one in context, then update them all to "Start free trial". leave tests and the changelog alone

prompts.title: Draft a document from past examples
prompts.content: read the privacy impact assessments in legal/pia/ to learn the structure and voice, then draft a new one for the new analytics integration

prompts.title: Write tests, run them, fix failures
prompts.content: write tests for app/parsers/feed.py, run them, and fix any failures

prompts.title: Drive implementation from tests
prompts.content: write tests for the password reset flow first, then implement it until they pass

prompts.title: Fill gaps from a coverage report
prompts.content: read coverage/coverage-summary.json and add tests for the lowest-covered files until each is above 80%

prompts.title: Migrate a pattern across the codebase
prompts.content: migrate everything from the old logging API to the structured logger: identify every place that needs to change, then make the changes

prompts.title: Port code to another language
prompts.content: port this Python module to Rust, keeping the same public API and test behavior

prompts.title: Optimize against a measurable target
prompts.content: optimize the search query to bring p95 latency from 2s down to under 500ms

prompts.title: Fix a precise visual bug
prompts.content: the login button extends 20px beyond the card border on mobile. fix it.

prompts.title: Review your changes before you commit
prompts.content: review my uncommitted changes and flag anything that looks risky before I commit

prompts.title: Review a pull request
prompts.content: review PR #247 and summarize what changed, then list any concerns

prompts.title: Review infrastructure changes before applying
prompts.content: here is my Terraform plan output. what is this going to do, and is anything here going to cause problems?

prompts.title: Run a security review with a subagent
prompts.content: use a subagent to review src/api/ for security issues and report what it finds

prompts.title: Catch issues before formal review
prompts.content: review launch-post.md for unsupported claims, missing attributions, and brand-guideline issues and list anything I should fix before it goes to legal

prompts.title: Course-correct a wrong approach
prompts.content: that is not right: the function signature needs to stay backward-compatible. try a different approach

prompts.title: Narrow the scope of a change
prompts.content: that is too much. keep only the changes to the validation logic in src/forms/ and undo your other edits

prompts.title: Turn a correction into a rule
prompts.content: you keep using default exports when this project uses named exports. add a rule to CLAUDE.md so this stops happening

prompts.title: Resolve merge conflicts
prompts.content: resolve the merge conflicts in this branch and explain what you kept from each side

prompts.title: Commit with a generated message
prompts.content: commit these changes with a message that summarizes what I did

prompts.title: Open a pull request from a ticket
prompts.content: find the Linear ticket about the login timeout and open a PR that implements it

prompts.title: Draft release notes from git history
prompts.content: compare v2.3.0 to v2.4.0 and draft release notes grouped by feature, fix, and breaking change

prompts.title: Write a CI workflow
prompts.content: write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs the tests and deploys to staging on every push to main

prompts.title: Find and fix a failing test
prompts.content: the UserAuth test is failing, find out why and fix it

prompts.title: Investigate a reported error
prompts.content: users are seeing 500 errors on /api/settings. investigate and tell me what is going on

prompts.title: Fix a build error at the root
prompts.content: here is a build error. fix the root cause and verify the build succeeds

prompts.title: Investigate a production incident
prompts.content: the checkout endpoint started returning 500s an hour ago. check the logs, recent deploys, and config changes, then tell me the most likely cause

prompts.title: Diagnose from a console screenshot
prompts.content: here is a screenshot of the GCP Kubernetes dashboard. walk me through why this pod is failing and give me the exact commands to fix it

prompts.title: Query logs in plain English
prompts.content: show me all failed logins for the auth service over the past 24 hours. write the query, run it, and tell me what stands out

prompts.title: Analyze a data file
prompts.content: read @reports/q1-signups.csv, summarize the key patterns, and write the results to an HTML page with charts, then open it in my browser

prompts.title: Generate variations from performance data
prompts.content: read @ads-performance.csv, find the underperforming headlines, and generate 20 new variations that stay under 90 characters

prompts.title: Turn a recurring task into a skill
prompts.content: create a /ship skill for this project that runs the linter and tests, then drafts a commit message

prompts.title: Add a hook for repeat behavior
prompts.content: write a hook that runs prettier after every edit to a .ts or .tsx file

prompts.title: Connect a tool with MCP
prompts.content: set up the Sentry MCP server so you can read my error reports directly

prompts.title: Capture what to remember for next time
prompts.content: summarize what we did this session and suggest what to add to CLAUDE.md
